abstract.text: Common assumptions can break when your app is used by a global audience. Learn about the many aspects of creating apps for different regions and languages. Understand how to use fonts and typography, layout techniques, and support text input so your app shines in all languages.

metadata.title: Creating Apps for a Global Audience

where you can search by language and it displays all the (installed) fonts that support that language.

inlineContent.text: macOS Mojave has a new “word-of-the-day” screensaver
For word emphasis, do not use italics, it doesn’t work in most of the languages, and if you use numbers, those will be the only thing italicized in your text 👎🏻. Bold is a better alternative.
For character emphasis, use different colors (bold doesn’t work for structural languages such as Hindi and Korean), use

abstract.text: Get details about new features of Auto Layout in Xcode, Cocoa and Cocoa Touch. Explore the new NSGridView, allowing your Cocoa app to easily build grid interfaces. Dive deeper into new tools that enable you to quickly diagnose layout issues in your interface. Learn about the new features in Xcode to quickly build adaptive interfaces.

With Dynamic Type, people choose their preferred text size and iOS switches fonts automatically as needed. Understand why Dynamic Type is important and how to support it when displaying text. Learn what’s new in iOS 11, and master the frameworks and tools that make it easy to support Dynamic Type in your app.
Session notes shared by the community for the community.

doc://WWDCNotes/documentation/WWDCNotes/.title: Choosing the Right Cocoa Container View

abstract.text: AppKit offers numerous ways to easily present your data. Join our framework engineers for a guided tour of versatile standard view classes you can put to work in your own macOS apps.  Hear about NSStackView, NSTableView, NSCollectionView, and other container views in AppKit.  Explore the interesting features and benefits of each, and examine real-world use cases to help you choose the most suitable building blocks for your apps’ user interfaces.

Get a glimpse inside to find out what happens when you add a constraint! Dive into the internals of Auto Layout to develop intuition for how your code affects what happens under the hood. Learn how to measure and refine your approach to Auto Layout and see how its improved performance in iOS 12 will speed up your app.

doc://WWDCNotes/documentation/WWDCNotes/.title: What’s New in International User Interfaces

abstract.text: Building a great international user interface goes beyond just translating your app. Explore new and improved APIs in iOS, macOS, and watchOS, and learn how Xcode 8 makes it easy to provide directional images to build first class apps for RTL languages. Hear from experts on how to fine-tune your international UI to create a seamless bidirectional experience and take your app in a new direction.
Xcode 10, Swift 4.2, iOS 12, macOS 10.14 (Mojave), tvOS 12, watchOS 5.

Following some simple practices when writing your app can help make it world-ready. Learn about what’s new, how to take greater advantage of existing frameworks, and review some common pitfalls and how to avoid them. Hear some ideas for going one step further and optimizing your app for different countries.

By understanding the range of abilities and capabilities of the people who use our apps, you can design robust apps that work for everyone. Learn how designing for accessibility and inclusiveness can do social good, widen participation, and enable everyone to benefit.

Leverage the abilities of TextKit to provide the best experience possible displaying and editing text. Get the best performance out of your app by using TextKit effectively. Learn the concepts to do more complex handling, layout and presentation.

Choosing a custom font for your app can be a daunting task involving both functional and stylistic decisions. Gain a solid understanding of fundamental font design characteristics such as proportion and contrast. Learn how to apply this knowledge when deciding which font is right for your app.

doc://WWDCNotes/documentation/WWDCNotes/.title: How to Pick a Custom Font

abstract.text: Typography is essential to an app’s form and function. Learn principles of good typography and techniques to apply them to your work. Make the most of the advanced features in our system fonts, and learn best practices for using custom fonts.

The localization process in Xcode 10 has been updated to aid you through the lifecycle of exporting, importing, and testing localized content in your apps. See new functionality added in Xcode 10 and learn how the new localization catalog helps you build world-class, localized apps.
